title: Wall Street closes its best quarter since 2020 with strong gains
summary: US stock indices record their biggest quarterly advance in six years, driven by economic optimism and the rebound of big tech.

title: Yen falls to 39-year low and Tokyo Stock Exchange surges
summary: The Japanese currency weakens to levels not seen in four decades, while the Nikkei index advances in its best stock market quarter in years.

title: South32 sells its aluminum portfolio to Alcoa for up to $5.6 billion
summary: The Australian miner divests alumina and bauxite assets, strengthening Alcoa's position in the global aluminum market.

title: Lime raises $167 million in its US IPO
summary: The shared scooter and bicycle company, backed by Uber, debuts on the US stock market with an offering valued at $167 million.

title: Bitcoin falls 20% in worst month in four years
summary: The leading cryptocurrency drops below $60,000, weighed down by regulatory uncertainty and the advance of artificial intelligence.

title: Gold heads for worst quarter in more than a decade
summary: The precious metal suffers a significant decline due to the strengthening dollar and reduced expectations of rate cuts.

title: The Magnificent Seven loses $2.3 trillion in stock market
summary: Major US tech companies suffer a sector rotation that wipes out trillions from their market value in the quarter.

title: Oil falls on fears of oversupply and forecast cuts
summary: Morgan Stanley warns of a possible crude oil glut, while oil futures retreat on expectations of increased production.

title: Acciona celebrates five years on the stock market and announces dividend
summary: The Spanish energy company marks its fifth anniversary of listing on the stock exchange and will distribute a dividend of 5.748 euros per share.

title: Major AI investments from Amazon and Blackrock
summary: Amazon injects $1 billion into a new AI unit, while Blackrock doubles down on artificial intelligence and recommends government bonds.

title: Warren Buffett suspends donation to Gates and advises calm amid falls
summary: The legendary investor pauses his philanthropy towards the Bill Gates foundation and recommends investors keep calm in times of volatility.

title: Donald Trump earns more than a billion dollars and plans Republican convention
summary: The former US president reports income exceeding one billion dollars in the last year and announces an unprecedented Republican convention before the midterm elections.

title: US Supreme Court blocks firing of a Fed governor
summary: The high court rules that the president cannot arbitrarily remove members of the Federal Reserve, guaranteeing their independence.

title: Binance and its founder sued by investors in London
summary: A class action lawsuit in London accuses the cryptocurrency exchange and Changpeng Zhao of regulatory violations.

title: BP vice president leaves the company amid restructuring
summary: The departure of the senior executive occurs at a time of changes at the British oil company to adapt to the energy transition.

title: Rio Tinto agrees new financial terms for $18 billion copper mine
summary: The Anglo-Australian miner renegotiates the conditions of its megaproject in Mongolia, valued at $18 billion.

title: Volatility in battery metals futures grows
summary: Prices of metals such as lithium and copper record strong fluctuations due to changes in demand for electric vehicles.

title: UK bond market remains stable after new prime minister
summary: Despite the change of government, UK bonds reflect fiscal continuity and little political reaction.

title: Ibex 35 closes best half-year in years with a rise of 12.5%
summary: The Spanish stock index exceeds 19,400 points driven by the good performance of banking and tourism.

title: German inflation falls in June more than expected
summary: The consumer price index in Germany stands below projections, easing pressure on the ECB.
